TSLA: Tesla's Austin Robotaxi Project Is Smaller Than Elon Musk's Claims: Report. A recent report indicates that Tesla's Robotaxi operations in Austin, Texas, are much smaller than CEO Elon Musk's claims, utilizing only 32 Model Ys in its network, far short of the promised 1,000. An engineering student's reverse-engineered app tracker revealed the service's limited availability and a smaller fleet compared to competitors like Waymo in Austin. Meanwhile, Waymo has reported significant progress with 450,000 weekly rides and a larger estimated fleet of 2,500 vehicles.

European stocks trade mixed; Novo Nordisk climbs, Tesla Europe sales dip. European stocks showed mixed performance in early Tuesday trading. Novo Nordisk shares rose significantly after its GLP-1 obesity pill secured FDA approval, while Tesla's European sales declined in November, contributing to the varied market activity. Other key developments included increased oil production in Norway, Cevian Capital raising its stake in Akzo Nobel, and Ryanair receiving a substantial fine from Italian regulators for market abuse.

Tesla Europe sales fall nearly 12% in Nov, rival BYD gains more ground. Tesla's European sales declined by nearly 12% in November, while Chinese rival BYD saw significant growth, increasing its market share. This downturn reflects Tesla's struggles with heightened competition and brand image issues, contrasting with BYD's successful expansion and popular hybrid offerings.

The Zacks Analyst Blog Alphabet, Tesla, Sony, Tredegar and CVD Equipment. This Zacks Analyst Blog highlights research reports on 16 major stocks, including Alphabet, Tesla, and Sony, along with micro-cap companies Tredegar and CVD Equipment. The reports detail financial performance, market positioning, and future outlooks for each company, covering aspects like AI integration for Alphabet, EV market dynamics for Tesla, and segment-wise growth for Sony. The article also touches upon the unique research Zacks provides for small and under-the-radar companies.

Elon Musk's Tesla Registers Over 1,500 Vehicles For California Ride-Hailing Fleet: Report. Tesla has reportedly registered over 1,500 vehicles for its ride-hailing fleet in California, adding more than 1,655 vehicles since the service launched in August. This expansion comes as Tesla tests driverless robotaxis in Austin, and amid challenges including a license suspension in California and misleading marketing claims regarding its Autopilot and FSD features. The company has not applied for a driverless permit in California, unlike rivals Waymo and Zoox.

Cathie Wood’s ARK sells Tesla stock, buys Oklo and Rocket Lab. Cathie Wood's ARK ETF significantly adjusted its portfolio on December 22, 2025, by selling a large portion of its Tesla stock and divesting from Rocket Lab. Concurrently, ARK made substantial investments in Oklo Inc., a nuclear energy company, and increased holdings in several biotechnology firms such as Pacific Biosciences, CRISPR Therapeutics, and Intellia Therapeutics, alongside purchases in autonomous and aviation sectors. These strategic moves highlight ARK’s focus on innovative energy solutions and reallocations within its technology and biotech investments.

Cathie Wood’s ARK sells Tesla stock, buys Oklo and Rocket Lab By Investing.com. Cathie Wood's ARK ETF has significantly reshuffled its portfolio, selling 60,715 shares of Tesla Inc (NASDAQ:TSLA) for over $29 million, continuing a trend of reducing its stake in the electric vehicle maker. Concurrently, ARK made substantial investments in innovative energy solutions by acquiring 107,321 shares of Oklo Inc (NASDAQ:OKLO) and divesting from aerospace company Rocket Lab Corp (NASDAQ:RKLB), selling 232,425 shares. The fund also adjusted its biotech holdings and invested in autonomous and aviation sectors.

A carpenter from Sooke, B.C., claims that he turned $88,000 into $415 million, before losing it all | CBC News. A carpenter from Sooke, B.C., claims he turned $88,000 into $415 million trading Tesla stock options but lost it all due to "inadequate advice" from RBC. RBC denies wrongdoing, stating that Christopher DeVocht did not heed advice to diversify and divest his multi-million dollar portfolio, and that he was a sophisticated and experienced options trader. The bank alleges he was committed to his "own all or nothing leveraged options trading strategy" which led to his losses.

Tesla Stock (TSLA) After Hours Today (Dec. 22, 2025): Record-High Momentum, Robotaxi Headlines, and What to Watch Before Tuesday’s Open. Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA) closed Monday's session near record highs, driven by optimism around its autonomy narrative, particularly robotaxi developments, and the restoration of Elon Musk's 2018 pay package. The market is increasingly valuing Tesla as an autonomy and AI story rather than just an automaker, with significant attention on "unsupervised" robotaxi testing in Austin and the broader implications of rival Waymo's recent outage-related stumbles. Investors are now looking toward Q4 2025 delivery numbers, expected in early January, as the next critical "hard-number" catalyst amidst continued robotaxi headlines and holiday-week trading volatility.

Winners And Losers Of Q3: General Motors (NYSE:GM) Vs The Rest Of The Automobile Manufacturing Stocks. This article reviews the Q3 earnings of several automobile manufacturers, highlighting General Motors (NYSE:GM), Ford (NYSE:F), Lucid (NASDAQ:LCID), Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA), and Rivian (NASDAQ:RIVN). It examines their revenue performance against analyst expectations and the market's reaction, noting the impact of evolving EV technology on the industry. The piece also provides a brief market update on the economy and recent political events.

Tesla Wins Transfer of Suit Over Self-Driving Tech to California. A Texas judge has ruled in favor of Tesla Inc., allowing a lawsuit regarding alleged patent infringement related to self-driving technology to be transferred to Northern California. This decision overturns an earlier denial after Tesla introduced nine new California-based witnesses, which ultimately swayed the judge's decision.

Tesla Stock (TSLA) News Today (Dec. 22, 2025): Musk Pay Package Ruling, Robotaxi Momentum, Analyst Forecasts, and the Key Risks Ahead. Tesla's (NASDAQ: TSLA) stock is trading near record highs on December 22, 2025, driven by the Delaware Supreme Court's restoration of Elon Musk's 2018 compensation plan, now valued at $139 billion, and ongoing robotaxi developments. While analyst forecasts show a wide range with many targets below current prices, the market remains focused on Tesla's long-term autonomy potential despite near-term headwinds like regulatory scrutiny from the California DMV and softer EV demand in the U.S. and Europe. Investors are closely watching Q4 delivery numbers and further robotaxi progress as key determinants for future stock movement.

Tesla: Delaware Supreme Court Reinstates CEO Musk’s 2018 Pay Package. The Delaware Supreme Court overturned a lower court's ruling that had voided Tesla CEO Elon Musk’s 2018 pay package, causing Tesla shares to rise. Morningstar maintains its $300 fair value estimate for Tesla, viewing the shares as overvalued but noting the reinstated pay package reduces key-person risk regarding Musk's departure. The compensation package, entirely share-based, is seen as crucial for retaining Musk and aligning with his desire for increased ownership.
TGT: Target, Walmart, Whole Foods targeted in botulism suits. Target, Walmart, and Whole Foods are being added as defendants in lawsuits against baby formula maker ByHeart. The lawsuits, filed by foodborne-illness attorney Bill Marler, allege that ByHeart sold a product potentially contaminated with botulism spores, leading to hospitalizations of 51 babies. The grocery chains are being targeted because ByHeart is likely to go bankrupt, and the retailers are expected to "step up — morally and legally — to help these families."

e.l.f. Beauty (NYSE:ELF) Stock Rating Upgraded by Wall Street Zen. Wall Street Zen upgraded e.l.f. Beauty (NYSE:ELF) from a "sell" to a "hold" rating, though other analysts have mixed views with a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and a target price of $124.53. The stock is currently trading below analyst expectations, opening at $80.39 with a market cap of $4.79 billion. Despite beating EPS estimates, the company missed revenue expectations in its last quarterly report and saw a significant insider sale by its CEO.

Christmas Eve 2025: Store hours for Costco, Walmart, Walgreens, Target, Best Buy and more. This article provides a comprehensive list of Christmas Eve 2025 operating hours for numerous major retailers in the U.S., including Walmart, Costco, Target, and Best Buy. It highlights that most stores will have reduced hours on Christmas Eve and many will be closed on Christmas Day. The article also touches on last-minute shopping options like same-day delivery and pickup services.

What stores are open in AL on Christmas Eve, Christmas Day 2025?. This article provides a guide for Alabama shoppers wondering which stores will be open on Christmas Day 2025. It details the Christmas Day and Christmas Eve hours for major retailers like Walmart, Target, Sam's Club, Costco, Publix, and Kroger, noting that most will be closed on Christmas Day. The piece also lists other grocery and hardware stores that will be closed.

Tanger Outlets, Kohl's will offer extended holiday hours this week. See when. Malls and retailers like Tanger Outlets, Kohl's, Target, TJ Maxx, and Walmart are extending their hours this week to accommodate last-minute holiday shoppers. The San Marcos Tanger Outlets will have extended hours but will be closed on Christmas Day, resuming normal hours on December 26th. Other major retailers will also operate with longer hours, mostly closing late on December 24th and remaining closed on Christmas Day.

Target, Walmart, Whole Foods Targeted in Botulism Suits. Target, Walmart, and Whole Foods are being added as defendants in lawsuits against baby formula maker ByHeart, related to a product potentially contaminated with botulism spores. Foodborne-illness attorney Bill Marler stated that these retailers need to "step up – morally and legally – to help these families" as ByHeart faces potential insolvency. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention reported 51 babies hospitalized with infant botulism linked to ByHeart formula, prompting the FDA to direct stores to improve recalled product removal efforts.

Bel Fuse B stock hits all-time high at 178.67 USD. Bel Fuse B Inc. (NASDAQ:BELFB) stock reached an all-time high of $178.67, with the company currently trading at $179.06, reflecting strong year-over-year and year-to-date gains. This surge follows impressive Q3 2025 financial results, where Bel Fuse surpassed earnings and revenue expectations, leading Oppenheimer to raise its price target. The company also declared quarterly cash dividends for its Class A and Class B common shares.

CTO Realty Growth signs anchor lease, reaches 100% occupancy. CTO Realty Growth, Inc. announced it has signed an anchor lease at its Marketplace at Seminole Towne Center property in Orlando, Florida, bringing the center to 100% leased occupancy. This 10-year lease for 48,000 square feet with an investment-grade national retailer is part of the company's strategy to strengthen its property portfolio. The company stated it is on track to achieve a significant positive cash leasing spread across its targeted anchor spaces.

Is Walmart, Target open on Christmas? What stores are open, closed in Mass. on Dec. 25. This article provides information on which major retail stores will be open or closed in Massachusetts on Christmas Day. Most large retailers like Walmart, Target, and Costco will be closed, while many convenience and food service establishments like CVS, Walgreens, Starbucks, and McDonald's will have varied or reduced hours, advising customers to check local listings. Several other major stores, including ALDI, Home Depot, and Whole Foods, are also confirmed to be closed for the holiday.

Were you among the Target shoppers affected by the outage? Check your email. Target is sending $20 eGiftCards to some shoppers who were impacted by a global technical outage during the holiday season. The outage on Friday caused issues with app orders, returns, and curbside pickup, leading to frustration among customers. Target has apologized for the disruption and confirmed that normal operations have resumed.

Target Cuts Production Times, Keep Its Fashion Moving at Internet Speeds. Target Corp. is accelerating its private-label fashion production by implementing "speed tracks" and utilizing a proprietary GenAI trend platform called "Target Trend Brain." This initiative aims to deliver trendy products faster to meet consumer demand, particularly among Gen Z and Millennial shoppers. The retailer has already seen success with this approach, exemplified by quickly launching baggy leopard-print jeans and expanding its faux suede assortment, driving significant incremental sales.

Bloomberg Reports: Target, Walmart, Whole Foods Targeted in ByHeart Botulism Suits. Target, Walmart, and Whole Foods are being added as defendants in lawsuits against baby formula maker ByHeart due to the sale of infant formula potentially contaminated with botulism-causing spores. Foodborne-illness attorney Bill Marler is expanding the lawsuits, arguing that these retailers must take responsibility given ByHeart's likely insolvency and the hospitalization of 51 babies with infant botulism linked to the formula. The FDA has also issued letters to Target and Walmart for failing to remove recalled ByHeart products from shelves.

Target, Walmart, Whole Foods targeted in ByHeart botulism suits. Target Corp., Whole Foods Market, and Walmart Inc. are being added as defendants in lawsuits against baby formula maker ByHeart. The lawsuits stem from a botulism outbreak linked to ByHeart's formula, which has hospitalized 51 babies. Foodborne-illness attorney Bill Marler plans to include these retailers, arguing they need to step up morally and legally as ByHeart may face insolvency.

Target, Whole Foods and Walmart to be added to ByHeart baby formula lawsuits. Target Corp., Whole Foods Market, and Walmart Inc. are facing legal action as they will be added as defendants in lawsuits against baby formula manufacturer ByHeart. The lawsuits allege that ByHeart's formula was potentially contaminated with botulism-causing spores, leading to the hospitalization of 51 infants. Foodborne-illness attorney Bill Marler plans to include these retailers, citing ByHeart's likely insolvency and the retailers' responsibility to the affected families.

Stores open on Christmas Eve and Christmas Day for last minute shopping. While many major retailers like Target and Walmart will be closed on Christmas Day, several convenience stores and pharmacies such as Circle K, Cumberland Farms, Walgreens, and Wawa will remain open. Most stores will have reduced hours on Christmas Eve, emphasizing the need to check local listings.
SBUX: What's Driving the Market Sentiment Around Starbucks Corp?. Starbucks Corp's short interest has decreased by 3.81% according to its latest report, with 43.08 million shares sold short, representing 3.79% of its float. This decline suggests a potentially more bullish sentiment among investors, though it doesn't guarantee a near-term stock rise. Compared to its peers, Starbucks has lower short interest, with the average peer group at 10.57%.

Quick Service Restaurant Market Set for Rapid Growth to Reach US$ 635.2 billion by 2032: Global Industry Forecast - McDonald's Corporation, Subway IP LLC, Yum! Brands Inc, Tata Starbucks Private Limited, Domino's Pizza Inc. The global Quick Service Restaurant (QSR) market is projected to grow from US$ 450.1 billion in 2024 to US$ 635.2 billion by 2032, exhibiting a CAGR of 4.4%. Key players like McDonald's, Subway, and Yum! Brands are driving growth through initiatives such as AI-driven kiosks, digital 'ghost kitchens', and plant-based menu items. The report provides a comprehensive analysis of market segments, regional performance, and key competitive strategies within the QSR industry.

Starbucks names former Amazon exec. Anand Varadarajan chief technology officer. Starbucks has appointed Anand Varadarajan, former Amazon VP, as its new executive vice president and chief technology officer. Varadarajan brings nearly two decades of experience from Amazon, Motorola, and Oracle, and will report directly to CEO Brian Niccol. He succeeds Deb Hall Lefevre and interim CTO Ningyu Chen.
DIS: Avatar: Fire and Ash’ Burns Bright at the Box Office with $347.1 Million Global Opening. 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' garnered an estimated $347.1 million globally in its opening weekend, securing the No. 1 spot both domestically ($89 million) and internationally ($258.1 million). This third installment in the Avatar franchise has been well-received by audiences, achieving a 91% Rotten Tomatoes Verified Moviegoers score and significantly boosting the overall franchise gross to $5.6 billion. The film also marked a historic opening in China, further contributing to Disney's strong box office performance in 2025.
NKE: Academy Sports and Outdoors Stock (ASO) Drops on Dec. 22, 2025: Nike Shockwaves, Q3 Results, Updated Guidance, Analyst Price Targets, and 2026 Forecasts. Academy Sports and Outdoors (ASO) stock declined on December 22, 2025, primarily due to sector-wide pressure following disappointing results from Nike, which impacted athletic retail exposure. Despite ASO's recent Q3 fiscal 2025 report showing improved profitability, narrowed full-year outlook, and gross margin expansion, investor caution persists regarding comparable sales and brand-driven demand signals. Analysts maintain a "Hold" consensus, with price targets indicating potential upside but also reflecting unresolved fundamental debates in the retail sector.
